field_label: LLM serving state; KV/cache; attention; token-memory bottlenecks
boundary_risks: Text clusters mix LLM inference with generic DNN accelerators; attention/HLS signals do not prove a separate field; 2025-2026 frontier evidence still needs artifact/adoption audit.
residual_cluster_notes: Use SF01 as an independent check for inference/accelerator vocabulary. Treat SF11 and SF98 LLM-looking rows as mixed residuals because they sit inside analog/RF/CMOS or cross-domain clusters. SF14 is boundary only and should not seed an LLM field.
field_worker_notes: Start from cross-venue inventory row GSF-01. Use textual clustering only to audit missing LLM inference/attention/KV rows and possible internal splits among serving state; attention kernels; quantization/runtime.

field_label: Neural rendering; NeRF; 3DGS; scene-reconstruction pipeline bottlenecks
textual_support_candidates: none_clean;SF01:medium weak accelerator substrate signal only
boundary_risks: Textual clustering does not form a clean neural rendering candidate; image sensor, LiDAR, 3DIC, and general vision rows can create false positives; direct 3DGS evidence is recent.
residual_cluster_notes: Residual SF11/SF98 contain occasional neural rendering or image/3D titles but are low-confidence mixed clusters. They should be used for boundary audit and false-negative search, not for opening a separate graphics/vision/circuit field.
field_worker_notes: Field worker should distinguish neural rendering/3DGS/SLAM pipeline bottlenecks from CMOS image sensors, RF/LiDAR circuits, and generic 3D integration. Pull representative evidence from second-wave matrices, not textual residuals.

textual_support_candidates: SF01:medium:TC02 compute-in-memory accelerator signal;SF04:medium:TC12 memory/cache/sparse signal
boundary_risks: CIM/PIM text terms are interleaved with analog macros, device circuits, RF/ADC rows, and generic memory hierarchy rows; analog/digital CIM, DRAM/HBM PIM, and CXL/NDP remain split candidates.

field_worker_notes: Use SF01 and SF04 as sanity checks for CIM/PIM and memory-wall terminology, but keep official field boundary from GSF-03. Do not promote analog/RF/ADC bulk clusters into this field without second-wave evidence.

textual_support_candidates: SF06:medium:TC39 closure/placement/routing signal;SF07:medium:TC46 HLS/FPGA signal;SF01:medium weak tensor/dataflow signal
boundary_risks: Text clusters mix HLS, synthesis, design-space exploration, physical closure, approximation, and FPGA deployment; tool availability and generated code do not prove adoption or reproducibility.
residual_cluster_notes: SF98 has synthesis/exploration/compiler-like residual clusters and should be audited for missed tool papers. SF11 includes AMS generator/design rows but stays boundary_audit_only unless tied to accelerator closure evidence.
field_worker_notes: Field worker should use SF06/SF07 to audit HLS/compiler/closure coverage and watch for internal split between HLS/DSE, tensor compiler/codegen, hardware generators, and LLM-assisted RTL/verification.

field_label: Physical/PPA; routing; timing; chiplet; 3DIC; package closure
boundary_risks: Physical and package terms overlap with analog/RF circuits, 3D integration, NoC, memory macros, and general EDA closure; 3DIC/package must not be confused with 3D scene reconstruction.
residual_cluster_notes: SF11 and SF98 contain chip/package/interconnect-looking titles but are mixed low-confidence groups. They are boundary audit material only and cannot become a separate RF/ADC/analog field in third wave.
field_worker_notes: Field worker should keep workload-agnostic physical closure separate from neural rendering 3D and from datacenter fabric. Use SF06 as an audit hook for placement/routing/timing/PPA rows.

field_label: Low-precision; sparsity; LUT/native arithmetic; approximate neural execution
textual_support_candidates: SF01:medium:TC02/TC08/TC26 quantized/approximate/neuromorphic signals;SF07:medium:TC46 approximate/HLS signal;SF04:medium weak sparse matrix signal

field_worker_notes: Field worker should separate low precision, sparsity, LUT/native arithmetic, approximate execution, and neuromorphic boundary rows. Use textual candidates for split-risk audit only.

boundary_risks: Graph, sparse tensor, sparse linear algebra, point cloud, MoE-like routing, and general cache/matrix terms remain mixed; text clustering may hide several internal splits.

field_worker_notes: Field worker should test split candidates among graph/GNN, scientific sparse linear algebra, point-cloud/3D sparse workloads, and MoE-like irregularity using paper evidence matrices rather than cluster labels alone.

field_label: Benchmark; profiling; simulator; artifact; evidence-validity infrastructure
boundary_risks: Textual clustering does not isolate benchmark/profiling/artifact infrastructure; simulator and verification terms are scattered across mixed clusters. Tool availability must not be read as adoption or reproducibility.
residual_cluster_notes: SF98 is the main residual audit pool for simulator/profiling/benchmark false negatives. SF11/SF14 should only flag boundary methodology rows embedded in circuits/biomedical/neuromorphic clusters.
field_worker_notes: Field worker should treat this as cross-cutting evaluation infrastructure. Do not convert every simulator/tool title into a field representative without second-wave claim/evidence linkage.

field_label: Edge robotics; sensor-near vision; SLAM; embodied low-latency perception
boundary_risks: Text clusters often capture sensors, image processors, autonomous vehicle/security, and neuromorphic rows rather than full embodied low-latency compute; direct robotics-policy/VLA evidence remains new.
residual_cluster_notes: SF11 contains sensor/image/RF rows and SF14 contains biomedical/neuromorphic/autonomous boundary rows. Both are boundary_audit_only. SF98 may contain edge/scheduling residuals but not a formal robotics field.
field_worker_notes: Field worker should separate sensor-near substrate, SLAM/vision pipelines, embodied edge inference, and newer VLA/robot-policy rows. Use textual clusters to find boundary false positives around sensors and neuromorphic systems.

boundary_risks: Datacenter fabric and orchestration signals are diluted across systems, cloud FPGA, NoC, cache, storage, and vendor disclosure rows; circuit/EDA venues provide weak direct support.

field_label: Security; correctness; reliability; verification constraints for accelerated systems
boundary_risks: Textual support is low-confidence and mixes security primitives, side-channel, homomorphic encryption, flash, verification, reliability, and boundary constraints; often not a primary accelerator-performance lineage.

field_worker_notes: Field worker should treat security/correctness/reliability as constraints around accelerated systems. Do not infer a mature performance lineage from SF09 alone; use second-wave claim ledgers for formal evidence.
